_meta.vertical: B2B — private jet charter operators, fractional / shared-ownership programs, concierge + travel-manager partners
_meta.audience: Decision-maker at private flight operator (founder, ops director, head of sales) — office hours, professional cell or main line
_meta.source_note: Aggregated from large B2B outbound datasets. Office-hours pattern. Validate per-account with your own dial logs.

core_windows.label: Morning planning window
core_windows.note: Golden hour around 8–9 or 9–10 a.m. — decision-makers are planning their day, not yet in back-to-back meetings.

core_windows.label: Late afternoon decision window
core_windows.note: Several B2B studies show 3–5 p.m. as one of the best windows to reach decision-makers and qualify.

experimental_blocks.label: Late executive window
experimental_blocks.note: Some operator owners and founders pick up after support staff has gone. Treat as experiment, not core block.

experimental_blocks.label: Monday afternoon recovery
experimental_blocks.note: If you must touch Monday, afternoon beats morning. Monday morning prospects are in catch-up mode.

avoid_windows.label: Lunch / midday crunch
avoid_windows.reason: B2B contact + qualification rates dip during the lunch/crunch hours.

avoid_windows.label: Monday morning
avoid_windows.reason: Prospects in catch-up mode, less open to new vendors.

avoid_windows.label: After-hours personal time
avoid_windows.reason: Travel + charter prospects guard personal hours. After 5:30-6 p.m. risks pushing into family time.

avoid_days.reason: Catch-up mode; weakest for new-vendor outreach.
Wind-down day; B2B contact rates drop.

homeowner_nuances: This is B2B HNW — your prospect is the operator or their sales lead, not the flier. Treat as classic office-hours prospecting.
Decision-makers at jet charter operators often travel on Mondays and Fridays. Mid-week reach is dramatically higher.
Concierge + travel-manager partners answer office lines + cells. After 5 p.m. you're likely catching personal time.
Aviation operators run 24/7 dispatch but the conversion-audit conversation is with sales leadership — strict 9-to-5.
Use first-name-only Tony variant per HNW discretion rules. No mascots, no real-photo Tony for ConversionJet.
